summary: Policy-backed control for app toast notifications. The local Microsoft WPN.admx file explicitly documents the same policy key, value name, and enabled/disabled values that the app uses.

setting.name: Toast Notifications
setting.casual_explanation: Toast notifications are the pop-up alerts apps show in Windows. This policy decides whether apps can raise those pop-ups for the current user.
setting.why_people_change_it: People disable toast notifications to reduce interruptions, keep the desktop quieter, or lock down notification behavior on managed devices.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Turning this off reduces distractions, but apps can no longer raise normal toast notifications for the current user.
setting.professional_notes: Microsoft documents NoToastNotification in ADMX_WPN. The app currently uses the user-side policy path HKCU\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\PushNotifications\NoToastApplicationNotification = 1, which matches the documented disable behavior. Microsoft also notes that this policy does not affect taskbar notification balloons and does not automatically disable every Windows system feature toast.

allowed_values.label: Not configured
allowed_values.meaning: Toast notifications stay enabled by default and can still be controlled by the administrator or user.

allowed_values.label: Toast notifications allowed
allowed_values.meaning: The policy is disabled and apps can still raise toast notifications.

allowed_values.label: Toast notifications turned off
allowed_values.meaning: Applications cannot raise toast notifications for the current user.

app_current_implementation.notes: The current app writes NoToastApplicationNotification = 1 for the current user, which matches the documented disable behavior.

windows_defaults.label: Windows user-policy default
windows_defaults.applies_to: Supported Windows 10 and Windows 11 editions with WPN policy support

states.rationale: The user policy is not configured by default.

windows_defaults.notes: Microsoft documents the functional default as toast notifications enabled when the policy is not configured.

recommended_profiles.label: Windows default
intended_for: People who want normal app notification behavior
recommended_profiles.good_when: You want apps to keep showing normal toast alerts.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want a quieter desktop or fewer pop-up interruptions.
tradeoffs: Apps can continue raising toast notifications.

recommended_profiles.label: Quiet notifications
avoid_for: Users who rely on app toast alerts

states.rationale: Block app toast notifications for the current user.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want app pop-up notifications suppressed.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You need apps to surface time-sensitive alerts through toasts.
tradeoffs: Some app alerts will no longer surface through the normal toast UI.

evidence.title: Microsoft Policy CSP: ADMX_WPN / NoToastNotification
evidence.summary: Official Microsoft documentation for the policy that turns off app toast notifications.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft WPN.admx NoToastNotification mapping
evidence.summary: The local ADMX file maps NoToastNotification to the PushNotifications policy key with enabledValue 1 and disabledValue 0.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft WPN.adml NoToastNotification help text
evidence.summary: The local ADML help text says enabling the policy turns off app toast notifications.

evidence.title: Current app implementation
evidence.summary: The provider writes NoToastApplicationNotification = 1 for the current tweak.

decision.why: The local Microsoft WPN.admx file explicitly defines NoToastNotification at SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\PushNotifications with valueName NoToastApplicationNotification and enabledValue 1 / disabledValue 0, which matches the app's policy surface.

validation_proof.notes: The local official Microsoft ADMX file names the same policy key and value name that the app writes and confirms the 1/0 mapping.
